heading: Sewer overflow and stormwater reuse municipal grants
label: 1301 Sewer overflow and stormwater reuse municipal grants

B, title I, § 112(d)]114 Stat. 2763Dec. 21, 2000“(1).—Report to congressNot later than 3 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency shall transmit to Congress a report summarizing—Dec. 21, 2000“(A)the extent of the human health and environmental impacts caused by municipal combined sewer overflows and sanitary sewer overflows, including the location of discharges causing such impacts, the volume of pollutants discharged, and the constituents discharged;“(B)the resources spent by municipalities to address these impacts; and“(C)an evaluation of the technologies used by municipalities to address these impacts.“(2).—Technology clearinghouseAfter transmitting a report under paragraph (1), the Administrator shall maintain a clearinghouse of cost-effective and efficient technologies for addressing human health and environmental impacts due to municipal combined sewer overflows and sanitary sewer overflows.”

B, title I, § 112(d)]114 Stat. 2763Dec. 21, 2000“(1).—Report to congressNot later than 3 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency shall transmit to Congress a report summarizing—Dec. 21, 2000“(A)the extent of the human health and environmental impacts caused by municipal combined sewer overflows and sanitary sewer overflows, including the location of discharges causing such impacts, the volume of pollutants discharged, and the constituents discharged;“(B)the resources spent by municipalities to address these impacts; and“(C)an evaluation of the technologies used by municipalities to address these impacts.“(2).—Technology clearinghouseAfter transmitting a report under paragraph (1), the Administrator shall maintain a clearinghouse of cost-effective and efficient technologies for addressing human health and environmental impacts due to municipal combined sewer overflows and sanitary sewer overflows.”

children.label: A
children.text: treatment works to intercept, transport, control, treat, or reuse municipal combined sewer overflows, sanitary sewer overflows, or stormwater;

children.label: B
children.text: notification systems to inform the public of combined sewer or sanitary overflows that result in sewage being released into rivers and other waters; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other measures to manage, reduce, treat, or recapture stormwater or subsurface drainage water eligible for assistance under .section 1383(c) of this title

children.heading: Direct municipal grants
children.label: 2 Direct municipal grants
children.text: Subject to subsection (g), the Administrator may make a direct grant to a municipality or municipal entity for the purposes described in paragraph (1).
is a municipality that is a financially distressed community under subsection (c);

has implemented or is complying with an implementation schedule for the nine minimum controls specified in the CSO control policy referred to in and has begun implementing a long-term municipal combined sewer overflow control plan or a separate sanitary sewer overflow control plan;section 1342(q)(1) of this title
is requesting a grant for a project that is on a State’s intended use plan pursuant to ; orsection 1386(c) of this title
is an Alaska Native Village.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 1 Definition
children.text: In subsection (b), the term “financially distressed community” means a community that meets affordability criteria established by the State in which the community is located, if such criteria are developed after public review and comment.

children.heading: Consideration of impact on water and sewer rates
children.label: 2 Consideration of impact on water and sewer rates
children.text: In determining if a community is a distressed community for the purposes of subsection (b), the State shall consider, among other factors, the extent to which the rate of growth of a community’s tax base has been historically slow such that implementing a plan described in subsection (b)(2) would result in a significant increase in any water or sewer rate charged by the community’s publicly owned wastewater treatment facility.

children.heading: Information to assist States
children.label: 3 Information to assist States
children.text: The Administrator may publish information to assist States in establishing affordability criteria under paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of activities carried out using amounts from a grant made under subsection (a) shall be not less than 55 percent of the cost.

children.heading: Rural and financially distressed communities
children.label: 2 Rural and financially distressed communities
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, the Administrator shall work with States to prevent the non-Federal share requirements under this subsection from being passed on to rural communities and financially distressed communities (as those terms are defined in subsection (f)(2)(B)(i)).

children.heading: Types of non-Federal share
children.label: 3 Types of non-Federal share
children.text: The applicable non-Federal share of the cost under this subsection may include, in any amount, public and private funds and in-kind services, and may include, notwithstanding , financial assistance, including loans, from a State water pollution control revolving fund.section 1383(h) of this title

children.heading: Administrative requirements
children.label: e Administrative requirements
children.text: A project that receives assistance under this section shall be carried out subject to the same requirements as a project that receives assistance from a State water pollution control revolving fund under subchapter VI of this chapter, except to the extent that the Governor of the State in which the project is located determines that a requirement of subchapter VI of this chapter is inconsistent with the purposes of this section. For the purposes of this subsection, a Governor may not determine that the requirements of subchapter VI of this chapter relating to the application of are inconsistent with the purposes of this section.section 1372 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $280,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2022 through 2026.

children.heading: Green projects
children.label: A Green projects
children.text: To the extent there are sufficient eligible project applications, the Administrator shall ensure that a State uses not less than 20 percent of the amount of the grants made to the State under subsection (a) in a fiscal year to carry out projects to intercept, transport, control, treat, or reuse municipal combined sewer overflows, sanitary sewer overflows, or stormwater through the use of green infrastructure, water and energy efficiency improvements, and other environmentally innovative activities.

children.heading: Rural or financially distressed community allocation
children.label: B Rural or financially distressed community allocation

children.heading: Financially distressed community
children.label: I Financially distressed community
children.text: The term “financially distressed community” has the meaning given the term in subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: Rural community
children.label: II Rural community
children.text: The term “rural community” means a city, town, or unincorporated area that has a population of not more than 10,000 inhabitants.

children.label: aa
children.text: treatment works to intercept, transport, control, treat, or reuse municipal sewer overflows, sanitary sewer overflows, or stormwater; or

children.label: bb
children.text: any other measures to manage, reduce, treat, or recapture stormwater or subsurface drainage water eligible for assistance under section 603(c).

children.heading: Rural communities
children.label: II Rural communities
children.text: Of the funds allocated under subclause (I) for the purposes described in that subclause, to the extent there are sufficient eligible project applications, the Administrator shall ensure that a State uses not less than 60 percent to carry out projects in rural communities.

children.heading: Fiscal year 2019
children.label: 1 Fiscal year 2019
children.text: Subject to subsection (h), the Administrator shall use the amounts appropriated to carry out this section for fiscal year 2019 for making grants to municipalities and municipal entities under subsection (a)(2) in accordance with the criteria set forth in subsection (b).

children.heading: Fiscal year 2020 and thereafter
children.label: 2 Fiscal year 2020 and thereafter
children.text: Subject to subsection (h), the Administrator shall use the amounts appropriated to carry out this section for fiscal year 2020 and each fiscal year thereafter for making grants to States under subsection (a)(1) in accordance with a formula to be established by the Administrator, after providing notice and an opportunity for public comment, that allocates to each State a proportional share of such amounts based on the total needs of the State for municipal combined sewer overflow controls, sanitary sewer overflow controls, and stormwater identified in the most recent detailed estimate and comprehensive study submitted pursuant to and any other information the Administrator considers appropriate.section 1375 of this title
the Administrator may retain an amount not to exceed 1 percent for the reasonable and necessary costs of administering this section; and

the Administrator, or a State, may retain an amount not to exceed 4 percent of any grant made to a municipality or municipal entity under subsection (a), for the reasonable and necessary costs of administering the grant.

children.label: i
children.text: recommended funding levels for grants under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the extent to which States pass costs associated with the non-Federal share requirements under subsection (d) to local communities, with a focus on rural communities and financially distressed communities (as those terms are defined in subsection (f)(2)(B)(i)).

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: The funding levels recommended under subparagraph (A)(i) shall be sufficient to ensure the continued expeditious implementation of municipal combined sewer overflow and sanitary sewer overflow controls nationwide.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 2 Use of funds
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Administrator shall submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report that describes the implementation of the grant program under this section, which shall include a description of the grant recipients, sources of funds for non-Federal share requirements under subsection (d), and grant amounts made available under the program.November 15, 2021
